-b: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}issue warnings about str(bytes_instance), str(bytearray_instance)and comparing bytes/bytearray with str.
-c: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}program passed in as string (terminates option list)
-d: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}turn on parser debugging output (for experts only, only works on debug builds);
-I^up: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}isolate Python from the user's environment (implies -E and -s)
-m: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}run library module as a script (terminates option list)
-P: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}don't prepend a potentially unsafe path to sys.path
-q: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}don't print version and copyright messages on interactive startup
-s: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}don't add user site directory to sys.path; also PYTHONNOUSERSITE
-u: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}force the stdout and stderr streams to be unbuffered
this option has no effect on stdin; also PYTHONUNBUFFERED=x
-v: also PYTHONVERBOSE=x can be supplied multiple times to increase verbosity
-V^up:
--version
{{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}print the Python version number and exit
when given twice, print more information about the build
-x: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}skip first line of source, allowing use of non-Unix forms of #!cmd
--help-env: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}print help about Python environment variables and exit
--help-xoptions: {{$PSCompletions.config.sym_opt}}print help about implementation-specific -X options and exit
--h: print this help message and exit (also -? or --help)
